- Wéi heescht Dir?
- Mäi Numm ass Sophie.
- Vu wou kënnt Dir?
- Ech kommen aus Lëtzebuerg.
- An Dir?
- Ech sinn aus Däitschland.
- Sidd Dir Schwäizer?
- Nee, ech sinn Fransous.
- Wéi al sidd Dir?
- Ech sinn 25 Joer al.
- Wéi geet et Iech?
- Et geet mir gutt, merci.
- Wat maacht Dir?
- Ech sinn Student.
- Wou wunnt Dir?
- Ech wunnen zu Lëtzebuerg.
- Hutt Dir eng Famill?
- Jo, ech hunn eng kleng Famill.
- Hutt Dir Kanner?
- Nee, ech hunn keng Kanner.
- Wéi laang sidd Dir schonn hei?
- Ech sinn zënter engem Joer hei.
- Wéi fënnt Dir d’Stad?
- Ech fannen d’Stad schéin.
- Wat ass Äre Lieblingsplaz?
- Mäi Lieblingsplaz ass de Park.
- Wat maacht Dir gär an der Fräizäit?
- Ech liesen gär Bicher.
- Hutt Dir eng Kaart?
- Jo, ech hunn eng Kaart.
- Wéi eng Kaart ass dat?
- Et ass eng Staterkaart.
- Firwat braucht Dir d’Kaart?
- Fir ëffentlech Verkéier ze benotzen.
- Wéi vill kascht d’Kaart?
- D’Kaart kascht 30 Euro am Mount.
- Wéi laang gëllt d’Kaart?
- D’Kaart gëllt fir e Mount.
- Kënnt Dir mir hëllefen?
- Jo, natierlech.
- Merci villmools!
- Gär geschitt!
- What is your name?
- My name is Sophie.
- And where are you from?
- I come from Luxembourg.
- And you?
- I am from Germany.
- Are you Swiss?
- No, I am French.
- How old are you?
- I am 25 years old.
- How are you?
- I am well, thank you.
- What do you do?
- I am a student.
- Where do you live?
- I live in Luxembourg.
- Do you have a family?
- Yes, I have a small family.
- Do you have children?
- No, I do not have children.
- How long have you been here?
- I have been here for a year.
- How do you like the city?
- I find the city beautiful.
- What is your favorite place?
- My favorite place is the park.
- What do you like to do in your free time?
- I like reading books.
- Do you have a map?
- Yes, I have a map.
- What kind of map is that?
- It is a state map.
- Why do you need the map?
- To use for public transport.
- How much does the map cost?
- The map costs 30 euros per month.
- How long is the map valid?
- The map is valid for one month.
- Can you help me?
- Yes, of course.
- Thank you very much!
- You’re welcome!

- Jo, dir könnt ihr schon allen zwei ein Stück Schokolade holen, me, dir darf nur drei Stück heimessen.

- Nee, dir würde lo genug Seisisch kreiten.
- Oh, guck mal, das sind Mickey Mouse.
- Kriegen wir schon?
- Jo, wir warten nicht.

- Guck mal, da gehen die Klüften verloren.
- Habt ihr gerne einen?
- Ja!
- What can he bake?
- What can they not?
- Listen that and cross it.

- No, you will not get a glass.
- Show me, there you can Mallen.

- Dad, can I also draw a piece of chocolate?
- Yes, you can both each a piece of chocolate, but you may only eat three pieces home.
- Dad, can I also grow a glow?

- Oh, look, those are Mickey Mouse.
- Do we get it already?
- Yes, we don’t wait.
- Oh, there are also Patien.

- No, also grab a Mickey Mouse.
- Dad, I have Geerknatsch.
- Can I draw a few?

- Look, there the cracks disappear.
- Do you like to have one?
- Yes!

- Umier.
- Wat gidd dir wann ich klift?
- Ech het gär Orange für Jütze machen.
- Ech jo ganz gut Orange aus Spanien.
- Si sinn ganz saftig, awer perfekt fir Jütze machen.
- Gidd mir zwee Kilo wann ich klift.
- Sonst nach eppes?
- Jo, ech kréien och nach Aprikosen.
- Wéi vill?
- Zéng Stéck.
- Sonst nach eppes?
- Jo, ech het gär Spargel wann ich klift.
- Oh, et tut mir leed. Ech hunn keen Spargel méi.
- Also, wéi vill kaschten Tomaten?
- Ech hunn Tomaten aus Spanien fir dräi Euro 49 de Kilo.
- An ech hunn Bio-Tomaten aus der Belsch fir véier Euro 75 de Kilo.
- Ech wëll e Kilo Bio-Tomaten.
- Ass dat alles?
- Jo, dat ass alles.

- Gudde Mettesch, wat fid dir Madame?
- Ech hätt gären hallef do se Briederscha an e Kärebrood, wann ech gelift.
- Geschnidden?
- Jo.
- Sos nach eppes?
- Jo, ech wellen och nach zwee Croissanten, zwee Erdäsche, ee Streisel an eng Schoklasroll.
- Et hätt ma leide, et déi keng Schoklasrolle mi do.
- Da gitt mir e Rang Schnéck.
- Ass dat alles?
- Nee, ech willen och nach eng Quetschentart.
- An dann ass et alles.
- Dat misst nun zéng Euro zwanzeg.
- Ech bezwille mat der Kaart.
- Gid dere Kudden, wann ech gelift.
- Voilà, hei ass er en Ticket.
- Merci an e schönen Dach.
- Merci gläichfalls.
- Adi.
- Good afternoon, madam, what would you like?
- I would like half a dozen bagels and a croissant, please.
- Cut?
- Yes.
- Any more?
- Yes, I would also like two croissants, two potatoes, one strudel and a chocolate roll.
- Unfortunately we don’t have any chocolate rolls here.
- Then give me a ring snick schnick.
- Is that all?
- No, I also want a lemon tart.
- And then that’s everything.
- That comes to ten euros twenty.
- I pay with card.
- Go ahead, please.
- Here is your ticket.
- Thank you and have a nice day.
- Thank you likewise.
- Bye.

- Ech mengen, fofzéng Tranche ginn duer.

- I will go to the butcher to buy meat.
- I think forty slices will be enough.

- Dëse Nove soll perfekt ginn.
- Dofir hunn ech nach vill ze dinn haut.
- Fir d'éischte ginn ech an de Supermarché an duerno bei de Metzler.
- Da brénge ech Saachen heem a staubsaugen de ganze Rezchossé.
- Um elef Auer hunn ech Rendezvous beim Coiffeur.
- Ech hätt gären eng schéin nei Kupp fir den Owend.
- Direkt beim Coiffeur ass e Blummebuttik.
- Do kafen ech nach e bësse Dekoratioun fir den Dësch.
- De Mëttel hunn ech da gutt Zeit fir den Haapplang an d'Obs ze preparéieren.
- Den Owend kann ech de Foma an de Gratin dann einfach am Schafche waarm maachen.
- Dann decken ech nach den Dësch an dann ass alles prett.
- Just ech nach net, mee da bleift jo nach genuch Zeit fir mëch schick ze maachen.
- This novel should be perfect.
- Therefore I still have a lot to do today.
- For the first I go to the supermarket and then to the Metzler.
- Then I bring things home and vacuum the whole dining room.
- At eleventh hour I have a haircut appointment.
- I would like a nice new outfit for the evening.
- Directly at the hairdresser is a flower shop.
- There I also buy a little decoration for the table.
- In the middle I have enough time to prepare the main course and the dessert.
- In the evening I can simply warm up the soup and the gratin in the oven.
- Then I set the table and everything is ready.
- Just I not yet, but there will still be enough time to get myself ready.

- Ech géif se kee Fleesch, awer leider hat mer nëmmen op de Guffen da Hamm,
- dofir konnt ech bal näischt vun der Entrée iessen.
- Den Haaptplaten hu mer ganz gutt fonnt,
- virun allem de Gratang huet mir gutt geschmaacht.
- Den Dessert war net extra an dobesonnesch vun der Spuenet gutt fonnt.
- Vun mir kritt Patrizia sechs Punkten.
- I would not eat any meat, but unfortunately I only had at the frozen yams,
- thus I could hardly eat anything from the starter.
- We liked the main courses very much,
- especially the gratin tasted good to me.
- The dessert was not extra and especially the Spaniard tasted good.
- From me Patrizia gets six points.

- Schwéier.
- Ech hunn alles immens gutt fonnt an och d'Drénge gutt gepasst.
- D'Glace war och nach selwer gemaach, Kombinatioun vu Mango a Banann war genial.
- Just de Bësse Salot war net extra.
- Ech ginn 9 vun 10.
- Heavy.
- I found everything extremely good and the drinks also fit well.
- The ice cream was also homemade, the combination of mango and banana was genius.
- Just the little salt was not extra.
- I give 9 out of 10.

- Draai.
- Dondre war e bësse einfach, mee ganz gutt.
- Mä Loun war gutt séiss a safteg.
- De Sommet mat der Champignonszooss war excellent.
- Just den Dessert hunn ech net sou immens fonnt.
- D'Glips waren net ganz frësch an d'Ananas waren net zäideg.
- Am grousse Ganzen huet et mir awer ganz gutt geschmaacht an et war e flotte Nuwwent.
- Dofir ginn ech aacht vun zéng Punkten.
- Departure.
- Dondre was a little easy, but very good.
- But the launch was pleasantly sweet and juicy.
- The mushroom sauce dish with the sauce was excellent.
- I did not find the dessert that amazing.
- The pastries were not quite fresh and the pineapples were not juicy.
- In general it tasted quite good to me and it was a nice evening.
- Therefore I give eight out of ten points.

- Faya!
- Also, et déet mir leed, Patricia, mee dat Iessen war wierklech net reeusséiert.
- Donte wäerte einfach den Häpp pla, pjo.
- Ech esse kee Fësch.
- De Grumperegratin war okay, mee just Grumperen a Spinat.
- Nou je, dat ass net immens.
- An den Dessär?
- Glassen hunn ech guer net probéiert.
- Ech esse aus Prinzip keng exotisch Friischten.
- An Dürkst halot?
- Pff, Dürkst war net frisch.
- Eng Nott?
- Faya vun Zéng.
- An dat ass nach ze vill.
- Faya!
- Hello, Patricia, but that meal really did not work out.
- You would just lay the snack there, please.
- I do not eat fish.
- The gratin was okay, but just croutons and spinach.
- Well, that’s not huge.
- And dessert?
- I have not tried sauces at all.
- I do not speak exotic fruits.
- And you? - this line unclear: "An Dürkst halot?" Might be "And you?" but with Dürkst? Could be "And you?" In Luxembourgish, "An Dir halot?" means "And you?" We'll translate as "And you?"
- Pff, Dürkst was not fresh.
- A note?
- Faya of ten.
- And that is still too much.

- Wëllt Dir nach eppes?
- Jo, ech hätt gär e Stéck Kuch.
- Dat ass gutt. Ech huelen och e Stéck Kuch.
- Villmools Merci!
- Gär geschitt!

- Would you like anything else?

- Guten Owend.
- Guten Owend.
- Hut dir reservéiert?
- Jo, mir hun en Dësch ob den Numm Wilmes reservéiert.
- Fir zwou Persoune?
- Jo.
- Voilà, hei ass ären Dësch.
- Hët dir gär en Aperitif?
- Jo, zwee Campari Orange wannescht gelift.
- Gären, hei sinn Menüskaarten.
- Ech brengen niesten Aperitif direkt.
- Hëls du eng Entrée oder just en Haapla?
- Oh, ech mengen, ech wöllen eng Entrée, entweder eng Zopp oder eng Zalot.
- Ech wölle de Foie Gras oder d'Skampien.
- An als Haapla?
- Ech wees et net.
- Fisch oder Fleesch?
- Ech wölle Fleesch.
- E Biftek, wie immer.
- Nee, ech wöll och de Poulet.
- Ech wöll en Pasteit oder Brochett.
- Hëls du och en Dessert?
- Dat wees ech nach net.
- Woll kucken.
- Wannesch gelift, zwee Campari Orange.
- Hut dir scho gewielt?
- Good evening.
- Good evening.
- Have you reserved a table?
- Yes, we have a table under the name Wilmes reserved.
- For two people?
- Yes.
- Here you go, here is your table.
- Would you like an aperitif?
- Yes, two Campari Orange, please.
- Certainly, here are the menus.
- I bring the aperitif right away.
- Would you like an entree or just a main course?
- Oh, I think I want an entree, either a soup or a salad.
- I want the foie gras or the scallops.
- And as a main?
- I don’t know.
- Fish or meat?
- I want meat.
- A steak, as always.
- No, I want also the chicken.
- I want a pastry or a sandwich.
- Would you also like a dessert?
- I don’t know yet.
- I’d like to watch.
- Please, two Campari Orange.
- Have you already chosen?

- Waat braucht Dir fir Bounechlupp ze kachen?
- Ech brauch Boune, Waasser, Salz a e bësse Peffer.
- An Dir?
- Ech brauch och Zwiebel a Knuewelek.
- Wéi laang muss d'Bounechlupp kachen?
- Et muss ongeféier eng Stonn kachen.
- A wéi eng Kachmethod benotzt Dir?
- Ech kachen et am Dëppen op niddreger Hëtzt.
- Ass et wichteg d'Boune virum Kachen ze wäschen?
- Jo, et ass wichteg fir Schmutz ewechzehuelen.
- Wat ass Äre Liiblingsbounechlupp-Zousaz?
- Ech léiwer et mat e bësse Speck.
- Villmools Merci fir d'Informatioun!
- What do you need to cook beans?
- I need beans, water, salt, and a little pepper.
- And you?
- I also need onions and garlic.
- How long do the beans have to cook?
- It has to cook for about an hour.
- And which cooking method do you use?
- I cook it in the pot on low heat.
- Is it important to wash the beans before cooking?
- Yes, it is important to remove dirt.
- What is your favorite bean soup topping?
- I prefer it with a little bacon.
- Thank you very much for the information!

- Waat braucht Dir fir Bounechlupp ze kachen?
- Ech brauch Boune, Waasser, Salz a e bësse Peffer.
- An Dir?
- Ech brauch och Zwiebel a Knuewelek.
- Wéi laang muss d'Bounechlupp kachen?
- Et muss ongeféier eng Stonn kachen.
- A wéi eng Kachmethod benotzt Dir?
- Ech kachen et am Dëppen op niddreger Hëtzt.
- Ass et wichteg d'Boune virum Kachen ze wäschen?
- Jo, et ass wichteg fir Schmutz ewechzehuelen.

- Ech léiwer et mat e bësse Speck.
- Villmools Merci fir d'Informatioun!

- Grompere schielen a graffrappen.
- Mam Miel an den Eeër en Deeg preparéieren.
- Mat Salz, Peffer, Muskat an de Kräider ofschmaachen.
- Den Deeg mat de gerappte Grompere mëschen.
- Ueleg an d'Pan maachen.
- Pro Grompere Kichelchen e Läffel vum Deeg an der Pan platt drécken.
- Kichelcher e puer Minutten op den zwou Säite broden, bis se gëlldechbrong sinn.
- Pickled onions with grated carrots.
- Prepare the eggs on the side for the day.
- Season with salt, pepper, nutmeg, and herbs.
- Mix the day with the grated carrots.
- Put oil in the pan.
- For each carrot cake, press a spoonful of the batter into the pan.
- Fry the cakes a few minutes on both sides until golden brown.
